howItWorks.intro: This cocktail cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Cost per Drink = (Bottle Cost / Bottle Oz × Spirit Oz) + Mixer Cost
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is the standard bar markup?
faq.answer: Bars typically mark up drinks 4-5x cost. A drink costing $2 to make would sell for $8-10. High-end bars may charge 6-8x markup.

faq.question: Is the Cocktail Cost Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What bottle cost should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate bottle cost value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Cocktail Cost Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

sections.title: What Is the Cocktail Cost Calculator?
sections.body: The Cocktail Cost Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your bottle cost, bottle size, spirit per drink, you get instant results including cost per drink, drinks per bottle, suggested bar price. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting cost per drink right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your bottle cost and need to find the right cost per drink. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Bottle Cost ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your bottle cost — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: bottle size, spirit per drink, mixer/garnish cost. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your cost per drink, drinks per bottle, suggested bar price. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your bottle cost before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both cost per drink and drinks per bottle — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your mixer/garnish cost, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
